name: L'Oréal
collaborationStyle: product launch, tutorial integration, ambassador
notes: Strong creator program across all sub-brands (Maybelline, NYX, Garnier, Lancôme); mid+ preferred

name: Sephora
collaborationStyle: haul, product review, brand ambassador, campaign
notes: Sephora Squad program makes this accessible to micro creators; one of the most creator-active beauty retailers

name: Estée Lauder
collaborationStyle: luxury product launch, skincare routine, editorial-style campaign
notes: Goes through agency; selective about creator aesthetic — prefers polished, editorial tone

name: Charlotte Tilbury
collaborationStyle: glam tutorial, product launch, get-ready-with-me
notes: Very active in creator space; accessible to mid-tier beauty creators with strong makeup content

name: Fenty Beauty
collaborationStyle: shade range showcase, complexion routine, campaign integration
notes: Rihanna's brand; perfect for creators highlighting inclusive shade ranges and bold looks

name: MAC Cosmetics
collaborationStyle: pro-level tutorials, collection launches, editorial glam
notes: Legacy pro brand; strong fit for creators with editorial or bold artistry looks

name: Huda Beauty
collaborationStyle: glam looks, palette launches, social-first campaigns
notes: Creator-founded brand with heavy social presence; ideal for bold makeup channels

name: Maybelline New York
collaborationStyle: drugstore haul, everyday makeup, mascara/foundation launches
notes: Mass drugstore brand; strong fit for creators who do affordable beauty content

name: Dior Beauty
collaborationStyle: luxury campaigns, red-carpet looks, fragrance launches
notes: Luxury house; best for highly polished luxury or fashion-beauty hybrid channels

name: Glossier
collaborationStyle: no-makeup makeup tutorials, skincare-first routines, aesthetic storytelling
notes: Strong alignment with natural, dewy, and aesthetic-focused beauty creators

name: e.l.f. Cosmetics
collaborationStyle: dupes, affordable haul, tutorial, TikTok-style challenge
brandPersonality: accessible, fun, Gen Z, cruelty-free, value-driven
notes: One of the most creator-native beauty brands; famous for viral campaigns; wide creator range accepted

name: Tatcha
collaborationStyle: skincare ritual, ingredient deep-dive, luxury routine
notes: Premium skincare with a loyal cult following; strong fit for skincare-focused and slow beauty creators

name: Rare Beauty
collaborationStyle: GRWM, mental health x beauty story, product showcase
notes: Selena Gomez's brand; resonates strongly with creators who blend beauty with personal/mental wellness content

name: Drunk Elephant
collaborationStyle: skincare routine, ingredient education, shelfie/routine showcase
notes: Hugely popular skincare brand on YouTube; high audience recognition drives strong conversion

name: Morphe
collaborationStyle: full glam tutorial, palette review, collab product launch
notes: Built its brand through creator collabs; very familiar with partnership workflow; accessible CPM

name: NARS
collaborationStyle: editorial makeup look, product spotlight, campaign integration
notes: Strong fit for creators with a fashion or editorial aesthetic; slightly more selective than drugstore brands

name: ColourPop
collaborationStyle: affordable hauls, collection launches, collab reviews
notes: Extremely active with creators; good for colorful, experimental, or budget beauty channels

name: Milk Makeup
collaborationStyle: stick products, clean looks, festival/creative content
notes: Best for creators with an editorial or experimental aesthetic who like cream and stick formats

name: The Ordinary
collaborationStyle: ingredient education, skincare science, routine building
notes: Strong fit for skincare educators and science-focused beauty channels

name: La Roche-Posay
collaborationStyle: dermatologist-style content, sensitive skin routines, SPF education
notes: Great for creators who collaborate with derms or do evidence-based skincare content

name: Anastasia Beverly Hills
collaborationStyle: brow tutorials, eye looks, palette launches
notes: Brow-first brand with strong name recognition; ideal for eye and brow-focused tutorials

name: Urban Decay
collaborationStyle: smoky eye looks, palette launches, edgy glam
notes: Best for edgier or colorful eye-focused creators; legacy Naked palette audience

name: Sol de Janeiro
collaborationStyle: body care routines, fragrance layering, summer content
notes: Fragrance and body care brand; a favorite in GRWM and body care routines

name: Soft Services
collaborationStyle: body skincare routine, ingredient spotlight, honest review
notes: Cult body skincare brand; prioritizes authenticity over reach — ideal for niche skincare creators

name: Tower 28
collaborationStyle: sensitive skin story, clean beauty routine, minimal makeup look
notes: SkinSafe-certified clean beauty brand; great for creators with sensitive skin or clean beauty audiences

name: Ami Colé
collaborationStyle: natural glam, skin-first makeup, representation-focused content
notes: Makeup brand designed for deeper skin tones; ideal for creators in the Black beauty or skin-first makeup space

name: Topicals
collaborationStyle: skin condition story, treatment review, before/after integration
notes: Skincare brand targeting hyperpigmentation and eczema; strong fit for creators who discuss skin conditions openly

name: Kulfi Beauty
collaborationStyle: South Asian beauty story, kajal/liner tutorial, cultural beauty content
brandPersonality: South Asian-inspired, joyful, bold, culturally rich
notes: Beauty brand rooted in South Asian beauty traditions; ideal for creators in the desi or multicultural beauty space

name: Kosas
collaborationStyle: clean makeup routine, skin-first look, ingredient education
notes: Fast-growing clean color cosmetics brand; strong fit for creators who blend skincare and makeup content

name: Youth To The People
collaborationStyle: superfood skincare routines, sustainable packaging content, ingredient stories
notes: Strong fit for eco-conscious skincare creators with a focus on ingredients

name: Summer Fridays
collaborationStyle: jet-lag beauty routines, aesthetic bathroom shelfies, clean girl looks
notes: Perfect for aesthetic-heavy beauty channels and GRWM content

name: Saie
collaborationStyle: no-makeup makeup routine, clean girl looks, hybrid skincare-makeup
notes: Great for creators doing natural, glowy editorial looks

name: Merit Beauty
collaborationStyle: five-minute face routines, minimalist beauty content, aesthetic hauls
notes: Ideal for creators leaning into quiet luxury and capsule wardrobe-style beauty

name: Freck Beauty
collaborationStyle: freckle tutorials, e-girl looks, playful content
notes: Perfect for experimental, alt, or e-girl makeup creators

name: Byoma
collaborationStyle: barrier routine content, drugstore skincare hauls, ingredient education
notes: Great for barrier repair and beginner drugstore skincare creators

name: Naturium
collaborationStyle: ingredient-led routines, comparison content, skincare education
notes: Susan Yara's brand; strong fit for skincare educators and routine creators

name: Live Tinted
collaborationStyle: color-correcting tutorials, hyperpigmentation content, representation-focused stories
notes: Excellent for creators focusing on hyperpigmentation and inclusive shade ranges
